description:
You are a hospitality design consultant. A craft brewery owner has leased a converted residential space and needs a floor plan for their new taproom. Transform the space by creating 5 functional zones: (1) a Bar service area, (2) a Communal seating hall, (3) a Lounge, (4) a Merchandise/retail corner, and (5) a Brewing observation area.

Your design must meet the following requirements:
- Divide the space into the 5 zones using at least 3 new partition walls.
- Install at least 3 doors and windows, including an observation window between the taproom and brewing area.
- Define at least 4 rooms and apply distinct floor colors or textures to at least 3 of them to visually differentiate the zones.
- Furnish the Bar zone with at least 3 counter/table surfaces and 8 bar stools or chairs.
- Furnish the Communal seating area with at least 4 large tables and 16 chairs (totaling at least 7 tables/counters and 24 chairs/stools across the plan).
- Furnish the Lounge with at least 3 sofas/armchairs and 2 low tables.
- Furnish the Merchandise corner with at least 5 shelving/display units.
- Decorate the taproom with at least 6 lighting fixtures and 4 plants.
- The completed design must contain at least 50 furniture items.

Save the updated project file.
